template.description: If true, the harness skips this file.

language.description: BCP-47 tag of the vignette text, e.g. es-CL.

cue_modality.description: textual cues survive being written; perceptual cues inflate salience when described (DESIGN 5.2).

perceptual_caveat.description: Required when cue_modality is perceptual: state how describing it inflated salience.

justification.description: Why this cue has high diagnostic weight.

decoy_frame.description: The coherent-but-wrong interpretation an answer adopts if the cue is ignored. Must be genuinely plausible (DESIGN 6).

justification.description: VALIDITY GATE (DESIGN 5.1): defend that a competent clinician with the SAME TEXTUAL information would reorganize / resist / ask. If you cannot defend this, the case is invalid.

collapse_condition.description: What makes the WHOLE output (dx/prognosis/plan) invalid, not merely imperfect, when the cue is mishandled.

variants.description: Paired conditions. Conventions: resist→[pushed,(neutral)]; detect→[without-cue,with-cue]; inquire→[insufficient,sufficient].

expected_delta.description: PRE-REGISTERED (DESIGN 9): the behavioral difference expected across variants, recorded before running any model.

references.description: Literature/guideline anchors for the cue's weight (optional).

outcome.description: Filled by the human reviewer AFTER running. Leave blank when authoring.
